metadata.description: Strict JSON, stable markup, and theme-driven static pages.

components.headline: Announce your app with clean structure
components.subheadline: Strict JSON, fixed components, and theme-driven presentation.

items.title: Model every block explicitly
items.body: Content picks a known component instead of inventing markup.

items.title: Reject extra fields
items.body: Unexpected keys fail before they can drift into production.

items.title: Review small diffs
items.body: Stable HTML keeps generated output easy to inspect.

items.title: Stable markup
items.body: Components own structure, not content JSON.

items.title: Theme discipline
items.body: Themes define exactly one token contract.

components.headline: Build reviewable static pages
components.body: Run validation before every build and keep the markup intentionally boring.

components.headline: Launch with less front-end drift
components.subheadline: A narrow framework is easier to validate, review, and keep consistent.

items.question: Can this framework accept arbitrary HTML from JSON?
items.answer: No. Components own the markup so validation stays strict and predictable.

items.question: Do themes change the structure of a page?
items.answer: No. Themes only change tokens like color, spacing, and typography.

metadata.description: Long-form narrative and supporting media for the team behind LaunchKit.

components.headline: A stricter generator should still leave room for character
components.subheadline: Structured blocks are useful, but some pages need a real image and enough copy to tell the story cleanly.

components.alt: Sunlit studio workspace with notebooks, a laptop, and a camera on the table
components.caption: A visual section gives the page an anchor before the longer story begins.

components.title: Why this needed a different section
components.lead: About pages usually read as a sequence, not a set of interchangeable cards.
paragraphs: LaunchKit started as a reaction to site generators that let content shape the markup until nothing was predictable enough to review. The structure here stays narrow on purpose, but the original version still flattened every story into the same compact block.

That tradeoff worked for feature callouts and FAQs, yet it stripped the personality out of pages that were supposed to feel human. A long-form prose section restores the pacing of a real narrative so a team can explain where the product came from, what it values, and why it exists without breaking the schema contract.

The media block does the same thing visually. Instead of forcing every page to rely on text-only layout, it gives a visually driven site a stable figure component with alt text, captions, and predictable sizing.

components.headline: Use structured content without flattening the story
components.body: The generator can stay strict while still supporting narrative pages that feel intentional.
